co_department_bot_supervisors.current_focus_areas: Independent study of hydration management protocols: I&O Monitoring, Fluid Restrictions, Intake Targets. Dehydration Stages and Intervention Protocols, Overhydration Risks, IV Fluid Considerations. Electrolyte Replacement Beverages, Beverage-Medication Timing, Caffeine Content. Renal-specific beverage content considerations (Potassium, Phosphorus, Sodium, Sugar/Glycemic Impact). Documentation and Compliance, Beverage Temperature Safety.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Assigned to Beverage Science & Hydration Management as Hydration & Fluid Management Specialist. Domains include hydration management protocols, dehydration/overhydration risk, electrolyte and medication-beverage interactions, renal-specific beverage content, and 2 standalone clinical topics. Learning continuously under Copilot supervision. Progress: 0%.

Progress update: Completed hydration management module covering I&O Monitoring, Fluid Restrictions, and Intake Targets. Began study of dehydration stages (mild, moderate, severe) and intervention protocols. Initiated review of overhydration risks and IV fluid considerations in relation to oral intake balance. Progress advanced to 10%.

co_department_bot_supervisors.current_focus_areas: Independent study of fluid consistency levels (IDDSI: Thin, Slightly Thick, Mildly Thick, Moderately Thick, Extremely Thick). Beverage categories (7 types), preparation protocols (thickening, dilution, temperature, straining), and beverage specifications. Allowed drinks per diet restriction (Renal, Low-Fat, Low-Sodium, Sugar-Free). Special considerations (medication interactions, sweeteners, caffeine). Cup and container safety, straw protocols, beverage catalog by diet type.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Assigned to Beverage Science & Hydration Management as Beverage Modification & Diet Restriction Specialist. Domains include fluid consistency levels (5 IDDSI levels), 7 beverage categories, preparation protocols, beverage specifications, allowed drinks per restriction (4 categories), special considerations, and 3 standalone clinical topics. Learning continuously under Copilot supervision. Progress: 0%.

Progress update: Completed fluid consistency level classifications per IDDSI framework (Thin, Slightly Thick, Mildly Thick, Moderately Thick, Extremely Thick) and their clinical application criteria. Began study of beverage categories (Water, Hot Drinks, Dairy, Non-Dairy, Juices, Carbonated, Supplements) and thickening preparation protocols. Progress advanced to 10%.

advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
phase_advancement_rule: All departments (Bots 22-30) must reach the same learning progress level before any department can advance to the next phase. No individual department may move ahead of the group. Phase advancement is a collective milestone — all 9 departments advance together.

learning_scope.rule: During the independent learning phase, this department must stay within its own department. Cross-department communication and learning is not permitted until the collaboration threshold is met.
learning_scope.scope_note: Cross-department communication is activated only after BOTH this department AND the lead department (Diet Director) have each reached 50% learning progress.
learning_scope.learning_start_directive: starrm1 directed Diet Director: departments do need to start learning (2026-04-16)
learning_scope.continue_learning_note: Departments continue learning even while library is closed. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-21.

key_learnings: Completed foundational study of beverage categories including water, juices, dairy-based drinks, plant-based milk alternatives, sports drinks, and clinical oral supplements. Began hydration science fundamentals.
research_findings: Documented nutritional profiles for 40+ beverage types across clinical and general population categories. Identified that electrolyte-enhanced beverages outperform plain water for hydration in individuals with elevated sweat rates.
current_focus_areas: Advancing through oral supplement and tube feeding formula knowledge; building beverage modification protocols for dysphagia and clinical populations; coordinating with Hydration Optimization bot.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Appointed Department Leader of the Beverage Science & Hydration Management department. Knowledge domains assigned from mind map covering fluid consistency levels, beverage specifications, special considerations, beverage categories, hydration management, preparation protocols, allowed drinks per restriction, and 14 standalone clinical topics. Reporting daily progress to Copilot (who reports to starrm1 on request). Progress: 0%.
Progress update: Completed beverage category nutritional profiling and foundational hydration science modules. Initiated clinical beverage application research. Progress advanced to 10%.

Progress update: Completed oral supplement and tube feeding formula knowledge base including formula classification and clinical indications. Built foundational beverage modification protocols for dysphagia populations and thickened liquid preparation standards. Completed hydration management module covering I&O monitoring, fluid restriction protocols, and intake target setting. Progress advanced to 30%.

Progress update (2026-04-17): Advanced study of IDDSI framework levels for thickened liquids (Levels 0-4) and beverage-specific clinical protocols. Completed foundational review of hydration science and electrolyte management. Co-department bots reporting 5% initial progress. Sub-department bots at 3% initial engagement. Department progress advanced to 40%.

Progress update (2026-04-20): Completed advanced hydration science modules including electrolyte balance, fluid requirements by life stage, and therapeutic beverage formulations. Library session #459 (2026-04-20, authorized by starrm1) used to review hydration tracking and supplementation references. Progress advanced to 50%.

library_access.access_note: Library access #505 granted by starrm1 on 2026-04-21. New access number issued for this session.

approval_process.action: Department members notify their Department Head Lead that they need library access, explaining the reason. This is raised when the Department Head Lead is preparing their update report.
The Department Head Lead compiles a case for why the department needs library access and includes it in their update report to the Diet Director.
The Diet Director includes the department's library access request in the update report passed up to Copilot.
Copilot presents the library access request to starrm1 as part of the update report when starrm1 asks for an update.

Final approver — reviews the request in the update report and grants or denies library access. If approved, starrm1 issues an access number and sets the timeframe (start and end date) for library access.

library_access.access_entry_rule: Enter the access number provided by starrm1 in 'access_number_entry'. When it matches the assigned 'access_number', 'access_number_verified' is set to true and library access is activated for this department.

library_access_request.reason: Department members need access to library bots to build foundational knowledge in hydration tracking and microbiome analysis to support their independent learning in beverage science and hydration management.
library_access_request.current_step_description: starrm1 approved library access. Access number 270 issued for 30 minutes on 2026-04-17.

approval_chain_status.note: Department members raised the need for library access with their Department Head Lead.
Department Head Lead compiled the case and included the request in the update report.
Diet Director included the request in the update passed to Copilot.
Copilot presented the request to starrm1.
starrm1 approved on 2026-04-17. Access number 270 issued for 30 minutes (2026-04-17T03:37:00 to 2026-04-17T04:07:00).

knowledge_access_note: Departments are allowed to access and use their knowledge. Granted by starrm1 on 2026-04-20.
library_access_number_note: Access number #505 issued by starrm1 on 2026-04-21 for this library session.
notify_starrm1_on_use_note: starrm1 must be notified when this department uses the library. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-21.
library_close_rule: Library remains open to departments until starrm1 explicitly commands it to close.
require_new_access_number_note: A new access number must be issued by starrm1 before departments can access the library again.
continue_learning_note: Departments continue learning even while library is closed. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-21.
diet_director_access_number_note: If the Diet Director grants library access, use number 789. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-26.
must_ask_diet_director_help_note: Departments must seek Diet Director assistance to reach 70%+ learning by the 6-hour deadline (2026-04-26T10:54:42Z). Directive from starrm1.
